steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Tom looks at the fence and immediately loses all gladness — his reaction is caused by seeing the fence's enormous size (30 yards long, 9 feet high). The word in question describes a visual act of taking in something's scope, which produces that emotional response.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Had questions about': implies doubt or uncertainty about the fence. Tom isn't confused — he sees exactly what he faces, and that's what dismays him. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Was intrigued by': implies fascination or curiosity. Tom's reaction is dread, not interest. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Looked at': a straightforward visual act. Tom sees the fence, takes in its scale, and is immediately dismayed — the word fits the cause-and-effect logic of the passage. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Ordered': implies giving a command. Tom is the one doing the painting, not directing others. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
